title: UIR L3 — application
description: Routes, the signal graph, stores, endpoints and design tokens. Docs, server and design generators consume this layer alone.

SignalScope.description:
Where a signal lives.

N11 (`promote-cross-route-state`, ADR-11) rewrites `component` to `store` when a signal or its mutator crosses a route boundary: a closure cannot be serialized to a URL, so the state must outlive the component that declared it.

x-uir-enum-docs.component: Owned by one component — a Flutter State field.
x-uir-enum-docs.store: Owned by a store, and therefore able to cross a route boundary.

x-uir-enum-docs.declared: The application declared it — e.g. a ChangeNotifier class.
x-uir-enum-docs.promoted: N11 synthesized it, to hold state that crosses a route boundary (ADR-11). Reported as BRG2302: promotion is never silent.

RouteArgumentTransport.description:
How a route argument survives — or fails to survive — a URL boundary (ADR-11, ADR-11a).

In Flutter a route argument is a live Dart value. In every URL-routed target it is a string in an address bar. This enum is where that difference is made explicit rather than discovered at runtime.

x-uir-enum-docs.primitive: A primitive. Crosses a URL boundary unchanged.
x-uir-enum-docs.promotedSignal: A signal read, promoted to a store by N11 and dropped from the transition (BRG2302).
x-uir-enum-docs.promotedCallback: A callback, promoted to a store action by N11 and dropped from the transition (BRG2302).
x-uir-enum-docs.objectTransport: A live object. A URL carries an identifier, not an object graph: reported as BRG2301 and left to the developer (evidence insufficient to infer identity and loader).
x-uir-enum-docs.unpromotable: A callback that captures state N11 cannot promote. Reported as BRG2303; an override is required.

MaterialRole.description:
A Material 3 colour role.

ADR-13: `hello_bridge` declares only `brightness`, `primaryColor` and `scaffoldBackgroundColor`; every other colour Flutter paints is *derived* algorithmically. That information is not in the Dart source text — it is in Material's algorithm — so N10 computes the full role set with `material_color_utilities`, the same package Flutter itself uses. The colours we copied verbatim matched exactly; the ones we guessed were wrong by up to 15/255 per channel, invisibly (M0-T5 F1).

x-uir-enum-docs.primary: The Material 3 `primary` colour role, derived from the seed and brightness by material_color_utilities (ADR-13). Never guessed.
x-uir-enum-docs.onPrimary: The Material 3 `onPrimary` colour role, derived from the seed and brightness by material_color_utilities (ADR-13). Never guessed.
x-uir-enum-docs.primaryContainer: The Material 3 `primaryContainer` colour role, derived from the seed and brightness by material_color_utilities (ADR-13). Never guessed.

TokenGroup.description: Which design-token family a token belongs to.

SeoMeta.description: Route metadata for the document head.

RouteArgument.description: One argument passed to a route transition, and what became of it.

name.description: The parameter name on the destination.

transport.description: How it survives the URL boundary.

binding.description: The value passed, as bound at the call site.

diagnostic.description: The diagnostic raised, when the argument cannot cross unchanged.

promotedTo.description: The store this argument's state was promoted into, for the promoted transports.

Signal.description:
A unit of reactive state.

Every target's reactivity is a lowering of this: React hooks, Vue `computed`, Svelte runes and Angular signals all come from here (ADR-4). No generator ever sees `setState`.

store.description: The store that owns it, when scope is `store`.

Derived.description: A value computed from other signals — a Flutter getter over state.

Effect.description: A side effect tied to a component's lifecycle.

Action.description:
A mutation of state — the normalized form of a `setState` body or a store method.

`writes` must include state mutated through **method calls** on owned collections (`add`, `remove`, `[]=`), not only assignments. C1 evidence: `FavoritesStore.toggle` mutates via `_favoriteIds.add/remove`, so an assignment-only analysis returns an empty write set — and generated React state that never updates.

Store.description: A collection of signals, derivations and actions that outlives any one component.

origin.description: Whether the application declared it, or N11 synthesized it.

layout.description: The layout component wrapping it, if any.

RouteTransition.description:
A navigation from one place in the application to another — a `Navigator.push`, or a `go_router` navigation.

This is the input to N11 (ADR-11). C1 found that `go_router` is the dominant navigation shape in real apps and our first analyzer saw none of it; with an empty nav-graph, N11 silently does nothing — a pass that looks like it works because it never fires.

**Exactly one of `target` and `component` is present** (Spec v2.4 §A17). A navigation either names a route that exists, or constructs its destination inline — and the second is not a route: it has no path, and none is invented for it. The dialect cannot express that exclusivity (a `NodeId` is a string; nothing about its shape says what it points at), so it is checked in code at emit and again at load — `BRG1307`.

A `Navigator.pop()` is **not** a transition: it returns along an edge that already exists rather than creating one.

target.description:
The destination route — an `app.Route` — when the navigation names one, as `context.go('/wonder/3')` does.

Absent when the navigation constructs its destination inline; see `component`.

component.description:
The `ui.Component` rendered, when the navigation constructs its destination inline — `Navigator.push(context, MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => HomeScreen()))`.

There is no path here and none is invented: which URL such a push *becomes* on a path-based target is a legalization decision, made in the layer that knows the target (Spec v2.4 §A17.6).

Absent when the navigation names a route; see `target`.

source.description: The component the navigation happens from.

Endpoint.description: A network call the application makes.

request.description: The request body type, if any.

Token.description:
One design token.

Colour tokens are **derived**, never guessed: see MaterialRole and ADR-13. INV-20: every colour a mapped Material widget paints resolves to a token, and generated code contains no literal colour values.

name.description: The token name within its group.

role.description: The Material colour role, for colour tokens.

light.description: The value in the light scheme.

dark.description: The value in the dark scheme, when the theme defines one.
